Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:02 ET
Extreme put flow erupts in single names despite positive gamma umbrella shielding indices
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Massive single-name put explosions (SMPL +10,200%, PSMT +1,540%) signal stock-specific stress despite SPY's +$4.9B positive gamma buffer
  • 2. Implied correlation plunges to 0.10 — lowest decile reading creates prime dispersion opportunity as single-name vol disconnects from index
  • 3. CPI at 8:30am represents critical volatility catalyst with VIX backwardation already pricing elevated near-term event risk
NARRATIVE — protected bearish - positive gamma

Single-name put demand exploded to extreme levels while index positioning remains insulated by dealer gamma protection. SMPL put volume spiked +10,200% and PSMT +1,540%, yet SPY maintains a massive +$4.9B positive gamma cushion that mechanically dampens directional moves — the ultimate structural divergence. This creates a regime where stock-specific shocks can amplify while broad market volatility stays contained, confirmed by implied correlation collapsing to 0.10. The vol surface reveals tactical opportunities as nine mega-caps trade in backwardation ahead of 8:30am CPI, with ARM, META, and MRVL pricing elevated near-term fear despite strong technical momentum. Notably, MRVL shows the strongest bullish technical score (88.7) with golden cross and RSI at 72, yet trades rich to its sector ETF — suggesting either informed hedging or technical momentum diverging from fundamentals. The June $150 calls block of 5,000 contracts at $4.35 aligns with this technical strength. Cross-asset positioning shows energy breaking out as XLE attracted 5,000 April $60 calls at $0.59, coinciding with oil volatility (OVX) remaining elevated. This sector rotation occurs as tech names like MSFT face death cross technicals despite Millennium's aggressive Q4 position build of +135% to $2.49B, creating a fundamental vs. technical tension that today's CPI could resolve.

Layer 1: Sentiment -13Layer 2: GEX +$4.9BLayer 4: IC 0.10Layer 9: CPI 8:30am
